analysis.reasoning: Core message: Russia-sponsored UN resolution against glorification of Nazism was approved, with only the US and Ukraine opposing; framed with remembrance/victory hashtags. RU1: Implicitly portrays the US and Ukraine as opposing an anti-Nazism measure, casting them negatively/immorally—this aligns with depicting the West and ally (Ukraine) as hostile/immoral. RU2: Suggests Russia advancing a UN resolution and gaining broad support, hinting at Russia promoting a more just multilateral stance versus Western opposition; this supports the idea of Russia leading/advocating an alternative to the West-centric line. RU3: By sponsoring a resolution against Nazism, Russia is shown as acting for a just cause; this positions Russia as a benevolent partner. RU4: The hashtags #Victory75 #WeRemember evoke WWII/anti-Nazi legacy, subtly celebrating Russian/Soviet historical role; this aligns with admiring Russian history. RU5: Not about current forefront achievements. RU6: Not cultural/nature content.
